tasks.description: Create devcontainer package with core helper functions - Currently no devcontainer package exists. Create wt/pkg/devcontainer/devcontainer.go with functions: HasDevcontainer(path string) bool to check if .devcontainer folder exists, GetPrimaryDevcontainerPath(path string) (string, error) to get primary project's .devcontainer path for copying, and scaffolding for CreateDevcontainer(path string) error that will be implemented in later tasks.

Implement function to copy template files to .devcontainer folder - Create copyTemplateFiles(targetPath string) error in devcontainer.go that copies setup.sh, setup-bash.sh, setup-agents.sh from .devcontainer folder of this project to targetPath/.devcontainer/. Also create .gitignore with '*' content to exclude everything in .devcontainer from git.

Create function to generate devcontainer.json with conditional worktree bind-mount and color customization - Implement generateDevcontainerJSON(targetPath string) error that creates devcontainer.json. Use the template from _ignore/DESIGN.md. For worktrees, replace the placeholder '<PLACEHOLDER-FOR-WORKTREE-GIT-FOLDER>' with actual bind mount: 'source=${localWorkspaceFolder}/../../<primary-project-name>/.git,target=${localWorkspaceFolder}/../../<primary-project-name>/.git,type=bind,consistency=cached'. For primary branches, remove that line entirely. Use workspace.IsWorktree and workspace.GetPrimaryProjectPath to determine context. For workbench.colorCustomizations, reuse workspace.GenerateColorFromPath, workspace.GetContrastingForeground, and workspace.AdjustColorBrightness to generate the same color scheme as local.code-workspace files.

Complete CreateDevcontainer main function - Implement the CreateDevcontainer(targetPath string) error function that orchestrates: 1) Check if .devcontainer already exists (skip if yes), 2) For worktrees with primary having .devcontainer, copy entire folder from primary, 3) Otherwise create new: call copyTemplateFiles then generateDevcontainerJSON. Return descriptive errors for each failure case.

Add devcontainer command to TUI view help text - In wt/pkg/tui/view.go around line 430-480, add '[i] devcontainer' command to the help text. Show this command only when .devcontainer folder does NOT exist (similar to how '[v] local.code-workspace' is conditionally shown). Use devcontainer.HasDevcontainer to check. Add to both ItemTypeProject and ItemTypeWorktree cases.

Add 'i' key handler in TUI model for devcontainer creation - In wt/pkg/tui/model.go Update function around line 780-790 (where 'v' key creates workspace file), add case for 'i' key to call createDevcontainer(). Model this after the createWorkspaceFile pattern around line 1260. Implement createDevcontainer() tea.Cmd that calls devcontainer.CreateDevcontainer and returns success/error messages. Add devcontainerCreatedMsg and handle it in Update to refresh the view.

End-to-end testing of devcontainer feature - Test the complete feature: 1) Test on primary branch - verify .devcontainer created with all files and no .git bind-mount, 2) Test on worktree with primary having .devcontainer - verify folder is copied, 3) Test on worktree without primary .devcontainer - verify new folder created with .git bind-mount pointing to correct primary path, 4) Verify command disappears after .devcontainer exists, 5) Test that 'i' key is ignored when .devcontainer already exists, 6) Verify workbench.colorCustomizations in devcontainer.json matches the colors in local.code-workspace file for the same project (both should use the same primary path for color generation).
